diagrams.title: Onto Function Mapping Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows a function from A={1,2,3,4} to B={a,b,c} that is onto
elements: Arrows showing each codomain element has at least one preimage
diagrams.purpose: Visualize definition: every codomain element has incoming arrow

diagrams.title: Not Onto Function
diagrams.description: Shows a function where element 'c' in codomain has no preimage
elements: Domain with arrows pointing only to a and b
Codomain element c with no arrows pointing to it
Range = {a,b} is proper subset of codomain {a,b,c}
diagrams.purpose: Contrast to understand when function fails to be onto

diagrams.title: Onto Composition Diagram
diagrams.description: Shows g∘f: A→C where f:A→B and g:B→C are both onto
elements: f arrows from A to B cover all B elements
g arrows from B to C cover all C elements
Resulting g∘f arrows from A to C cover all C elements
diagrams.purpose: Prove composition of onto functions is onto

diagrams.title: Pigeonhole Principle for Onto
diagrams.description: Demonstrates domain size requirement for onto functions
elements: n=3 pigeons (domain elements), m=2 holes (codomain elements)
n=2 pigeons, m=3 holes - at least one hole empty - impossible for onto
